title: Hierarchical Agent Groups
concept: Structuring teams with managers that review worker outputs before final submission.

exercises.prompt: In a sequential agent crew, what happens to a task after the first agent finishes?

options.text: It gets sent back to the first agent for review.
It moves to the next agent for further enhancement.

exercises.explain: In a sequential agent crew, tasks move from one agent to the next, allowing each to add their touch.

cards.title: What are Hierarchical Agent Groups?
cards.body: Hierarchical agent groups are teams structured with layers, where some agents manage and review the work of others before it is finalized.

cards.title: Hierarchical Structure
cards.body: A hierarchical structure organizes teams into levels, with managers overseeing and reviewing the work of their team members.

cards.title: Like a Company
cards.body: Imagine a company where employees work on projects and their managers check the work before it's sent out. This ensures quality and accuracy.

cards.title: Example of Hierarchical Groups
cards.body: Think of a restaurant kitchen: the sous-chef prepares ingredients, while the head chef checks the final dish before it’s served.

cards.title: Myth: All Agents Work Independently
cards.body: Some believe every agent works alone without guidance. In reality, many groups have leaders who help ensure tasks are done right.

cards.title: Visualizing Hierarchy
cards.body: Teams can be structured in layers. Here's a simple view:

exercises.prompt: What role does a manager have in a hierarchical agent group?

options.text: They review and verify work submitted by workers.
They randomly assign tasks without oversight.
They ignore the work of their team members.

exercises.explain: The manager's role is to review and verify the quality of the work done by their team, ensuring everything is accurate and ready.

exercises.prompt: In a hierarchical agent group, all members work independently with no management.
exercises.explain: This statement is false because hierarchical groups have managers who oversee and guide team members.

exercises.prompt: In a hierarchical agent group, a manager checks the ____ of their workers.
exercises.sentence: In a hierarchical agent group, a manager checks the work of their workers.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'work' because it reflects what the manager reviews before final submission.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a key feature of hierarchical agent groups?

options.text: All tasks are done by robots without any human input.
Managers review and verify outputs before final approval.
Every agent works on the same level with no hierarchy.

exercises.explain: The key feature is that managers review and verify outputs, ensuring quality and organization in the task workflow.

exercises.prompt: What key benefit does a manager bring to a hierarchical agent group?

options.text: They allow all tasks to be completed faster.
They ensure quality by reviewing work before it's finalized.
They reduce the number of workers needed for a project.
They create chaos among the workers.

exercises.explain: The correct answer is that managers ensure quality by reviewing work before it's finalized. This step is crucial for maintaining high standards.
